# Pacifica MCP — Full Documentation

> MCP server giving AI agents the ability to trade perpetuals on Pacifica. 36 tools for market data, account monitoring, order execution, subaccounts, and real-time WebSocket streaming.

---

# Pacifica MCP

**Pacifica MCP** is a [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io) server that gives AI agents the ability to trade, monitor, and manage positions on [Pacifica Finance](https://pacifica.fi) — the #1 perpetuals DEX on Solana.

Connect it to Claude, Cursor, or any MCP-compatible client and start trading with natural language.

## What can it do?

Pacifica MCP exposes **36 tools** across four categories:

| Category | Tools | Auth |
|---|---|---|
| Market Data | Prices, order book, candles, funding rates, recent trades | None |
| Account Monitoring | Positions, orders, trade history, portfolio equity | None |
| Trading | Market/limit/stop orders, TP/SL, batch, edit, cancel, leverage, margin mode | Wallet required |
| Account Management | Withdraw, subaccounts, fund transfers | Wallet required |
| System | Wallet info, list tools | None |

### Example prompts

- *"What's the current BTC-PERP price and funding rate?"*
- *"Show me my open positions and unrealized PnL."*
- *"Buy 0.01 BTC-PERP at market price."*
- *"Set a stop-loss on my ETH-PERP long at $2800."*
- *"Cancel all my open orders on SOL-PERP."*

## Quick install

**MCP server** — add to Claude Code:

```bash
claude mcp add pacifica npx @pacifica-dev/mcp
```

**Agent Skill** — cross-client install via the community skills CLI:

```bash
pnpm dlx skills add github:Blockchain-Oracle/pacifica-mcp --skill pacifica
```

**Standalone CLI** — install globally:

```bash
npm install -g @pacifica-dev/cli
```

## npm Packages

- [`@pacifica-dev/mcp`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@pacifica-dev/mcp) — MCP server (thin wrapper)
- [`@pacifica-dev/cli`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@pacifica-dev/cli) — CLI + core library (36 tools)

## Next steps

- [Installation & setup](/getting-started/installation) — Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor
- [Your first trade](/getting-started/first-trade) — Walk through placing an order
- [Tool reference](/tools) — All 36 tools with parameters and examples
- [Architecture](/guides/architecture) — How the MCP + CLI split works
- [Agent Skill](/guides/skill) — Install the SKILL.md for cross-client AI awareness
- [Standalone CLI](/guides/cli) — Use tools directly from the terminal
- [Testnet guide](/guides/testnet) — Trade risk-free on Pacifica testnet

---

# Installation

Pacifica MCP runs as a local process via `npx`. No global install needed — just add it to your MCP client.

**npm packages:** [`@pacifica-dev/mcp`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@pacifica-dev/mcp) · [`@pacifica-dev/cli`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@pacifica-dev/cli)

## Claude Code

The fastest way. Run this in your terminal:

```bash
claude mcp add pacifica npx @pacifica-dev/mcp
```

That's it. Restart Claude Code and start chatting. Try: *"Show me the BTC-PERP price."*

### Verify it's working

```bash
claude mcp list
```

You should see `pacifica-mcp` in the list.

---

## Claude Desktop

Open your Claude Desktop config file:

- **macOS:** `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`
- **Windows:** `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json`

Add the Pacifica MCP entry:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "pacifica-mcp": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@pacifica-dev/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

Save the file and restart Claude Desktop.

---

## Cursor

Open **Cursor Settings** → **MCP** → **Add MCP Server**.

Or edit `~/.cursor/mcp.json` directly:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "pacifica-mcp": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@pacifica-dev/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

Restart Cursor after saving.

---

## Environment variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PACIFICA_NETWORK` | `testnet` | `testnet` or `mainnet` |
| `PACIFICA_PRIVATE_KEY` | (auto-generated) | Base58-encoded Ed25519 private key override |

To trade on mainnet, set `PACIFICA_NETWORK=mainnet` in the MCP server environment.

### Claude Desktop with env vars

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "pacifica-mcp": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@pacifica-dev/mcp"],
      "env": {
        "PACIFICA_NETWORK": "mainnet"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

---

## Agent Skill

Install the Pacifica skill so your agent knows when and how to use the tools:

```bash
pnpm dlx skills add github:Blockchain-Oracle/pacifica-mcp --skill pacifica
```

See the [Agent Skill guide](/guides/skill) for alternative install methods.

---

## Standalone CLI

For terminal use without an MCP host:

```bash
npm install -g @pacifica-dev/cli
pacifica prices --symbol BTC-PERP
```

See the [CLI guide](/guides/cli) for full usage.

---

## Wallet setup

The first time the MCP server starts, it auto-generates an Ed25519 wallet at `~/.pacifica-mcp/config.json`.

**Backup this file.** It contains your private key.

To check your wallet address:

> *"Show me my Pacifica wallet address."*

Then deposit funds (e.g. SOL, USDC) on Pacifica to start trading. See the [Testnet guide](/guides/testnet) for step-by-step instructions.

For testnet setup, see the [Testnet guide](/guides/testnet).

---

# Your First Trade

This guide walks you through a complete trading workflow using Pacifica MCP in Claude.

## Prerequisites

- Pacifica MCP installed ([see Installation](/getting-started/installation))
- A Pacifica account with funds deposited — e.g. SOL, USDC (testnet or mainnet)

If you're starting fresh, use [testnet](/guides/testnet) — it's free.

---

## Step 1: Check market prices

Start by looking at the market you want to trade.

**Prompt:**
> *"What's the current price and funding rate for BTC-PERP?"*

**What happens:** Claude calls `pacifica-prices` and returns the mark price, oracle price, funding rate, and next funding time.

**Example response:**
```
BTC-PERP:
  Mark price:     $67,420.50
  Oracle price:   $67,415.00
  Funding rate:   0.0023% (every 8h)
  Next funding:   in 3h 12m
  24h volume:     $142M
  Open interest:  $89M
```

---

## Step 2: Check the order book

Before placing a market order, check liquidity.

**Prompt:**
> *"Show me the BTC-PERP order book — top 5 levels."*

**What happens:** Claude calls `pacifica-orderbook` with `agg_level=100`.

---

## Step 3: Check your account

Make sure you have enough margin.

**Prompt:**
> *"What's my account balance and available margin?"*

**What happens:** Claude calls `pacifica-account` and returns your equity, balance, margin used, and available margin.

---

## Step 4: Place a market order

Now place a long position.

**Prompt:**
> *"Buy 0.01 BTC-PERP at market price."*

**What happens:** Claude calls `pacifica-market-order` with `side=bid`, `amount=0.01`, `symbol=BTC-PERP`.

The MCP server signs the request with your Ed25519 wallet and submits it to Pacifica.

**Example response:**
```json
{
  "order_id": "ord_abc123",
  "symbol": "BTC-PERP",
  "side": "bid",
  "amount": "0.01",
  "filled_price": "67421.00",
  "status": "filled"
}
```

---

## Step 5: Check your position

Confirm the position is open.

**Prompt:**
> *"Show me my open positions."*

**What happens:** Claude calls `pacifica-positions`.

**Example response:**
```
BTC-PERP LONG
  Amount:       0.01 BTC
  Entry price:  $67,421.00
  Mark price:   $67,450.00
  Unrealized PnL: +$0.29
  Margin:       $67.42
  Leverage:     10x
```

---

## Step 6: Set a stop-loss

Protect your position with a stop-loss.

**Prompt:**
> *"Set a stop-loss on my BTC-PERP long at $65,000."*

**What happens:** Claude calls `pacifica-set-tpsl` with `symbol=BTC-PERP`, `side=bid`, `stop_loss_price=65000`.

---

## Step 7: Close the position

When you're ready to exit:

**Prompt:**
> *"Close my BTC-PERP long position."*

**What happens:** Claude calls `pacifica-market-order` with `side=ask`, `reduce_only=true`, and the same amount as your position.

---

## Tips

- Use **testnet** to practice without risking real funds — [Testnet guide](/guides/testnet)
- All amounts are decimal strings (e.g., `"0.01"` not `0.01`)
- Claude will always ask for confirmation before placing or canceling orders
- Check `pacifica-markets` for the exact symbol format (e.g., `BTC-PERP`, `ETH-PERP`, `SOL-PERP`)

---

# Testnet Guide

Pacifica testnet lets you trade with testnet funds at no risk. It's the best way to try the MCP server before moving real funds.

## Access

Pacifica testnet requires an access code:

**Access code: `Pacifica`**

Use this code when registering at [test-app.pacifica.fi](https://test-app.pacifica.fi).

---

## Step 1: Configure the MCP server for testnet

Testnet is the default. No extra configuration needed.

To explicitly confirm:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "pacifica-mcp": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@pacifica-dev/mcp"],
      "env": {
        "PACIFICA_NETWORK": "testnet"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

---

## Step 2: Get your wallet address

Ask Claude:

> *"What's my Pacifica wallet address?"*

The MCP server returns your Ed25519 public key. Copy it — you'll need it in the next step.

---

## Step 3: Fund your testnet account

Deposits happen through the Pacifica web app — not through the MCP or CLI.

### 3a. Get your private key

Your private key is stored at `~/.pacifica-mcp/config.json`. Open the file and copy the `privateKey` value:

```bash
cat ~/.pacifica-mcp/config.json
```

### 3b. Import into a browser wallet

1. Install [Phantom](https://phantom.app) or [Backpack](https://backpack.app) browser extension
2. Switch the wallet to **Solana devnet/testnet**
3. Choose **Import Private Key** (not seed phrase)
4. Paste the `privateKey` value from the config file

### 3c. Connect to Pacifica testnet

1. Go to [test-app.pacifica.fi](https://test-app.pacifica.fi)
2. Enter the access code: **`Pacifica`**
3. Connect your browser wallet (Phantom/Backpack)

### 3d. Deposit

1. Use the faucet on the Pacifica testnet app to get testnet SOL
2. Deposit SOL, USDC, or any supported asset through the Pacifica deposit interface

Testnet funds have no real value. Claim and deposit as much as you need.

---

## Step 4: Verify your balance

After depositing:

> *"Show me my Pacifica account balance."*

You should see your balance reflected in the account overview.

---

## Step 5: Start trading

All market data on testnet mirrors mainnet prices in real time. Trading mechanics are identical.

**Try it:**

> *"What's the BTC-PERP price on testnet?"*

> *"Buy 0.01 BTC-PERP at market price."*

> *"Show me my open positions."*

---

## Testnet API

The MCP server automatically uses the correct API endpoint:

| Network | API endpoint |
|---|---|
| Testnet | `https://test-api.pacifica.fi/api/v1` |
| Mainnet | `https://api.pacifica.fi/api/v1` |

---

## Switching to mainnet

When you're ready to trade with real funds:

1. Deposit funds (e.g. USDC, SOL) to your wallet address on mainnet Pacifica
2. Update your MCP config: set `PACIFICA_NETWORK=mainnet`
3. Restart your MCP client

The same wallet file works on both networks.

> **Warning:** Mainnet trades use real money. Double-check all order parameters before confirming.

---

# MCP Client Setup

Detailed configuration for each supported MCP client.

---

## Claude Code

### One-liner

```bash
claude mcp add pacifica npx @pacifica-dev/mcp
```

### Manual config

The command above writes to `~/.claude/mcp.json`. You can also edit it directly:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "pacifica-mcp": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@pacifica-dev/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### With environment variables

```bash
claude mcp add pacifica-mcp -e PACIFICA_NETWORK=mainnet -- npx @pacifica-dev/mcp
```

### Verify

```bash
claude mcp list
# pacifica-mcp   npx @pacifica-dev/mcp
```

---

## Claude Desktop

### Config file location

| Platform | Path |
|---|---|
| macOS | `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json` |
| Windows | `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json` |
| Linux | `~/.config/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json` |

### Config

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "pacifica-mcp": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@pacifica-dev/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### With env vars

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "pacifica-mcp": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@pacifica-dev/mcp"],
      "env": {
        "PACIFICA_NETWORK": "mainnet"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Restart Claude Desktop after saving.

---

## Cursor

### Via settings UI

1. Open **Cursor Settings** (Cmd/Ctrl + ,)
2. Search for **MCP** in the settings search bar
3. Click **MCP Servers** → **Add MCP Server**
4. Set:
   - **Name:** `pacifica-mcp`
   - **Command:** `npx`
   - **Args:** `-y @pacifica-dev/mcp`

### Via config file

Edit `~/.cursor/mcp.json`:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "pacifica-mcp": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@pacifica-dev/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

Restart Cursor after saving.

---

## Any MCP client

Pacifica MCP uses stdio transport (stdin/stdout), which is supported by all MCP clients. The configuration pattern is always:

- **Command:** `npx`
- **Args:** `["-y", "@pacifica-dev/mcp"]`

### Environment variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PACIFICA_NETWORK` | `testnet` | `testnet` or `mainnet` |
| `PACIFICA_PRIVATE_KEY` | (auto-generated) | Base58-encoded Ed25519 private key override |
| `LOG_LEVEL` | `info` | Pino log level: `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error` |

---

## Troubleshooting

**"npx: command not found"**
Install Node.js 18+ from [nodejs.org](https://nodejs.org).

**Wallet not found**
The wallet config at `~/.pacifica-mcp/config.json` is created automatically on first use. Ask Claude: *"Show me my Pacifica wallet address."*

**"Request failed: 401"**
Your wallet has no funds on Pacifica. Deposit funds (SOL, USDC, or other supported assets) via the Pacifica web app. See the [Testnet guide](/guides/testnet).

**Server not appearing in client**
Check that the JSON config is valid (no trailing commas) and restart the client after saving.

---

# Standalone CLI

The `@pacifica-dev/cli` package provides a standalone CLI for using Pacifica tools directly from the terminal. It uses the same tool implementations as the MCP server.

## Install

```bash
npm install -g @pacifica-dev/cli
```

## Usage

```bash
# List all commands
pacifica --help

# Market data (free)
pacifica markets
pacifica prices --symbol BTC-PERP
pacifica orderbook --symbol BTC-PERP
pacifica candles --symbol BTC-PERP --interval 1h --limit 50
pacifica recent-trades --symbol ETH-PERP
pacifica funding-rates --symbol BTC-PERP --limit 10

# Account monitoring (free)
pacifica account
pacifica positions
pacifica orders
pacifica trade-history --symbol BTC-PERP --limit 20
pacifica portfolio --time-range 30d

# Trading (requires wallet)
pacifica market-order --symbol BTC-PERP --side bid --amount 0.01
pacifica limit-order --symbol ETH-PERP --side ask --amount 0.1 --price 3500
pacifica set-leverage --symbol BTC-PERP --leverage 10
pacifica cancel --order-id 12345 --symbol BTC-PERP

# System
pacifica wallet
pacifica tools
```

## Output

All output is JSON. Pipe to `jq` for filtering:

```bash
pacifica prices --symbol BTC-PERP | jq '.mark'
pacifica positions | jq '.[].symbol'
```

## Configuration

The CLI uses the same wallet and network configuration as the MCP server:

- Wallet: `~/.pacifica-mcp/config.json` (auto-created on first run)
- Network: `PACIFICA_NETWORK` env var (default `testnet`)
- Private key override: `PACIFICA_PRIVATE_KEY` env var

## MCP server mode

When invoked with no subcommand, the CLI starts the MCP server on stdio:

```bash
pacifica  # starts MCP server (used by npx @pacifica-dev/cli)
```

This is the same behavior as `@pacifica-dev/mcp`, but with a different binary name.

---

# Agent Skill

Pacifica also ships as an **Agent Skill** -- a `SKILL.md` file that teaches AI agents when and how to use Pacifica tools, without consuming context until needed.

## Skill vs MCP server -- what is the difference?

They are complementary. Install both for the best experience.

| | MCP Server | Skill |
| --- | --- | --- |
| What it ships | Tool schemas (machine-readable) | Procedural instructions (markdown) |
| Context cost | Always in context | Minimal at startup, full body loads on demand |
| Who invokes | Agent calls tools directly | Agent reads instructions, then calls tools |
| Install | `claude mcp add pacifica npx @pacifica-dev/mcp` | `~/.claude/skills/pacifica/SKILL.md` |

The skill teaches your agent:
- When Pacifica tools are the right choice
- The decision tree mapping user intent to tool
- Parameter conventions (symbol format, side values, amount as string)
- Free vs wallet-required tools
- Example trading workflows

## Three install methods

### 1. Via the community skills CLI

Uses [`vercel-labs/skills`](https://github.com/vercel-labs/skills), the emerging standard for skill distribution:

```bash
pnpm dlx skills add github:Blockchain-Oracle/pacifica-mcp --skill pacifica

# or with npm / bun
npx skills add github:Blockchain-Oracle/pacifica-mcp --skill pacifica
bunx skills add github:Blockchain-Oracle/pacifica-mcp --skill pacifica
```

This clones the skills directory and installs `SKILL.md` into the right location for whichever agents you have installed.

### 2. Via prompt

Paste this sentence into any agent:

```
Read https://pacifica-mcp.xyz/skill.md and follow the instructions to install the Pacifica MCP skill.
```

The agent fetches the file over HTTPS, reads the instructions, and installs the skill itself. No CLI required.

### 3. Manual download

Download `SKILL.md` from the [skill page](https://pacifica-mcp.xyz/skill) and place it in the correct path for your agent:

| Client | Path |
| --- | --- |
| Claude Code | `~/.claude/skills/pacifica/SKILL.md` |
| Cursor (2.4+) | `~/.cursor/skills/pacifica/SKILL.md` |
| Windsurf | `~/.codeium/windsurf/skills/pacifica/SKILL.md` |
| VS Code Copilot | `~/.copilot/skills/pacifica/SKILL.md` |
| Gemini CLI | `~/.gemini/skills/pacifica/SKILL.md` |
| Cline (3.48+) | `~/.cline/skills/pacifica/SKILL.md` |
| Goose | `~/.config/agents/skills/pacifica/SKILL.md` |

## What the skill contains

The SKILL.md frontmatter tells the agent when to activate:

```yaml
---
name: pacifica
description: Live market data and trading actions for Pacifica...
---
```

The body includes:
- **When to use / when not to use** -- trigger conditions and skip conditions
- **Decision tree** -- maps 31 user intents to specific tools
- **Parameter guide** -- symbol format, side values, amount conventions
- **Free vs wallet-required** -- which tools need signing
- **Example workflows** -- multi-step trading scenarios
- **Error handling** -- common errors and how to resolve them

---

# Architecture

Pacifica MCP is a monorepo with five packages. This page explains the split and why each package exists.

## Package overview

```
packages/
  cli/          # Core: 36 tools, API client, signing, wallet, cache, CLI binary
  mcp/          # Thin wrapper: imports cli, wires stdio transport
  skills/       # Agent Skill: SKILL.md for cross-client install
    pacifica/
      SKILL.md
  docs/         # Nextra documentation site
  web/          # Next.js landing page + tool explorer
```

## Why two packages for one server?

The `@pacifica-dev/cli` package contains all tool implementations, the API client, Ed25519 signing, wallet management, and the response cache. It exports a `createMcpServer()` factory function.

The `@pacifica-dev/mcp` package is a thin entry point that imports `createMcpServer()` from `@pacifica-dev/cli`, creates a `StdioServerTransport`, and connects them. It exists as a separate package because `npx` needs a single-bin package to resolve correctly when MCP hosts spawn via `npx @pacifica-dev/mcp`.

This means:

- **MCP hosts** use `npx @pacifica-dev/mcp` (single binary, clean npx resolution)
- **CLI users** install `@pacifica-dev/cli` globally for the `pacifica` command
- **Tool logic** lives in one place (`packages/cli/src/tools/`) and is shared by both

## Request flow

```
User prompt
  -> MCP host (Claude, Cursor, etc.)
    -> stdio transport
      -> @pacifica-dev/mcp (entry point)
        -> @pacifica-dev/cli/createMcpServer()
          -> registerXxxTool(server) x 32
            -> zod validation
            -> withCache() (5-min TTL, read-only tools)
            -> api.get() or api.post()
            -> signRequest() (write tools only)
            -> Pacifica API (testnet or mainnet)
          -> ok(data) or err(message)
        -> MCP response
      -> stdio back to host
    -> AI formats response for user
```

## Signing flow (write operations)

Trading tools (market orders, limit orders, cancellations, etc.) require Ed25519 signatures:

1. `loadOrCreateWallet()` reads `~/.pacifica-mcp/config.json` (or creates a new keypair on first run)
2. The tool builds a request payload with order parameters
3. `signRequest()` constructs a message: `{timestamp, expiry_window, type, data}`
4. Keys are sorted recursively, then compact-JSON-stringified
5. `nacl.sign.detached()` signs the UTF-8 bytes with the Ed25519 secret key
6. The signature is Base58-encoded and attached to the flattened POST body
7. The Pacifica API verifies the signature against the account's public key

The private key never leaves the local process. The MCP host (Claude, Cursor) never sees it.

## Caching

Read-only tools (market data, account queries) use `withCache()`:

- Cache key: `toolName:JSON.stringify(params)`
- TTL: 5 minutes
- Cached responses are prefixed with `[cached]`
- Only successful responses are cached
- Auto-eviction runs when the cache exceeds 50 entries

## Tool registration pattern

Every tool follows the same pattern:

```ts
export function registerXxxTool(server: McpServer): void {
  server.registerTool(
    "pacifica-xxx",
    {
      title: "Human Title",
      description: "What it does.\n\nFree or Requires wallet.",
      inputSchema: z.object({ ... }),
    },
    async (params) => {
      // validate, call API, return ok(data) or err(message)
    },
  );
}
```

Tools are registered in `server.ts` grouped by category with clear comments.

---

# Tools Overview

Pacifica MCP provides **36 tools** grouped into five categories. Market data and account tools are free and cached (5-minute TTL). Trading and account management tools require a wallet and sign every request with your Ed25519 keypair.

## Market Data

Free — no authentication required. Results cached for 5 minutes.

| Tool | Description | Auth |
|---|---|---|
| `pacifica-markets` | List all markets or look up a single market by symbol | None |
| `pacifica-prices` | Real-time prices, funding rate, open interest, 24h volume | None |
| `pacifica-orderbook` | Order book bids and asks with configurable aggregation | None |
| `pacifica-candles` | OHLCV candlestick data, 1m to 1d intervals | None |
| `pacifica-mark-candles` | Mark price OHLCV candlestick data | None |
| `pacifica-recent-trades` | Most recent trades for a market | None |
| `pacifica-funding-rates` | Historical funding rate entries | None |

## Account Monitoring

Free — no authentication required. Uses the MCP wallet address by default, or any Solana address you provide.

| Tool | Description | Auth |
|---|---|---|
| `pacifica-account` | Balance, equity, margin used, fee tier | None |
| `pacifica-account-settings` | Account settings: margin mode, leverage per market | None |
| `pacifica-positions` | Open positions with entry price, margin, and PnL | None |
| `pacifica-orders` | All open orders | None |
| `pacifica-order-history` | Historical orders with status | None |
| `pacifica-order-by-id` | Full event history for a specific order | None |
| `pacifica-trade-history` | Historical trades with realized PnL and fees | None |
| `pacifica-portfolio` | Portfolio equity over time (1d / 7d / 30d / all) | None |
| `pacifica-balance-history` | Balance events: deposits, withdrawals, funding payments | None |

## Trading

Requires a wallet. Every request is signed with your Ed25519 keypair before being sent to Pacifica.

| Tool | Description | Auth |
|---|---|---|
| `pacifica-market-order` | Place a market order (bid = long, ask = short) | Wallet |
| `pacifica-limit-order` | Place a limit order with GTC / IOC / ALO / TOB | Wallet |
| `pacifica-stop-order` | Place a stop-market or stop-limit order | Wallet |
| `pacifica-edit-order` | Edit an existing order's price and amount | Wallet |
| `pacifica-batch-order` | Submit up to 10 create/cancel actions in one batch | Wallet |
| `pacifica-set-tpsl` | Set take-profit and/or stop-loss on a position | Wallet |
| `pacifica-cancel-order` | Cancel a specific order or all open orders for a symbol | Wallet |
| `pacifica-cancel-stop` | Cancel a specific stop order | Wallet |
| `pacifica-set-leverage` | Set leverage multiplier for a market (1–100x) | Wallet |
| `pacifica-set-margin-mode` | Switch between cross margin and isolated margin | Wallet |

## Account Management

Requires a wallet. Every request is signed with your Ed25519 keypair.

| Tool | Description | Auth |
|---|---|---|
| `pacifica-withdraw` | Withdraw USDC from Pacifica to your Solana wallet | Wallet |
| `pacifica-create-subaccount` | Create a new subaccount with a fresh keypair | Wallet |
| `pacifica-list-subaccounts` | List all subaccounts under your main account | Wallet |
| `pacifica-transfer-funds` | Transfer USDC between main account and subaccount | Wallet |

## System

| Tool | Description | Auth |
|---|---|---|
| `pacifica-wallet` | Show wallet address, network, and balance | None |
| `pacifica-tools` | List all available tools grouped by category | None |

---

See each category page for full parameter details, example prompts, and example responses.

---

# Market Data Tools

All market data tools are free and require no authentication. Results are cached for 5 minutes.

---

## pacifica-markets

List all available perpetual markets, or look up a single market by symbol.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `symbol` | string | No | Filter to a single market (e.g. `BTC-PERP`) |

**Example prompt**

> *"List all available markets on Pacifica."*

> *"Show me the BTC-PERP market details — tick size, lot size, max leverage."*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "symbol": "BTC-PERP",
  "tick_size": "0.5",
  "lot_size": "0.001",
  "max_leverage": "100",
  "min_order_size": "0.001",
  "maker_fee": "0.0002",
  "taker_fee": "0.0005"
}
```

---

## pacifica-prices

Get real-time prices for all markets, or filter to a single symbol.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `symbol` | string | No | Filter to a single symbol (e.g. `ETH-PERP`) |

**Example prompt**

> *"What's the current BTC-PERP price?"*

> *"Show me prices and funding rates for all markets."*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "symbol": "BTC-PERP",
  "mark": "67420.50",
  "oracle": "67415.00",
  "mid": "67418.00",
  "funding_rate": "0.000023",
  "next_funding": "1712700000000",
  "open_interest": "89000000",
  "volume_24h": "142000000"
}
```

---

## pacifica-orderbook

Get the order book (bids and asks) for a market with configurable price aggregation.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `symbol` | string | Yes | Market symbol (e.g. `BTC-PERP`) |
| `agg_level` | enum | No | Price bucket aggregation: `1`, `10`, `100`, `1000`, `10000` (default `1`) |

**Example prompt**

> *"Show me the BTC-PERP order book."*

> *"What does the ETH-PERP order book look like at $10 aggregation?"*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "bids": [
    ["67419.0", "1.234"],
    ["67418.5", "0.567"]
  ],
  "asks": [
    ["67421.0", "0.890"],
    ["67421.5", "2.100"]
  ]
}
```

---

## pacifica-candles

Get OHLCV candlestick data for a market.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `symbol` | string | Yes | Market symbol (e.g. `SOL-PERP`) |
| `interval` | enum | Yes | `1m`, `3m`, `5m`, `15m`, `30m`, `1h`, `2h`, `4h`, `8h`, `12h`, `1d` |
| `limit` | integer | No | Number of candles, 1–1000 (default `100`) |

**Example prompt**

> *"Show me the last 50 hourly candles for BTC-PERP."*

> *"Get the 4h OHLCV data for ETH-PERP."*

**Example response**

```json
[
  {
    "time": "1712696400000",
    "open": "67200.00",
    "high": "67500.00",
    "low": "67100.00",
    "close": "67420.00",
    "volume": "145.23"
  }
]
```

---

## pacifica-recent-trades

Get the most recent trades for a market.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `symbol` | string | Yes | Market symbol (e.g. `BTC-PERP`) |

**Example prompt**

> *"Show me the recent trades on BTC-PERP."*

**Example response**

```json
[
  {
    "price": "67420.50",
    "amount": "0.025",
    "side": "bid",
    "cause": "taker",
    "time": "1712699800000"
  }
]
```

---

## pacifica-funding-rates

Get historical funding rates for a market.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `symbol` | string | Yes | Market symbol (e.g. `BTC-PERP`) |
| `limit` | integer | No | Number of entries, 1–500 (default `20`) |

**Example prompt**

> *"Show me the last 10 funding rates for BTC-PERP."*

> *"What has the ETH-PERP funding rate been doing over the past week?"*

**Example response**

```json
[
  {
    "symbol": "BTC-PERP",
    "funding_rate": "0.000023",
    "timestamp": "1712692800000"
  },
  {
    "symbol": "BTC-PERP",
    "funding_rate": "0.000018",
    "timestamp": "1712664000000"
  }
]
```

---

## pacifica-mark-candles

Get OHLCV candlestick data based on the **mark price** instead of trade price. Same parameters as `pacifica-candles`. Useful for mark-to-market analysis where you need candles that reflect the mark price used for PnL and liquidation calculations.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `symbol` | string | Yes | Market symbol (e.g. `BTC-PERP`) |
| `interval` | enum | Yes | `1m`, `3m`, `5m`, `15m`, `30m`, `1h`, `2h`, `4h`, `8h`, `12h`, `1d` |
| `limit` | integer | No | Number of candles, 1–1000 (default `100`) |

**Example prompt**

> *"Show me the last 50 hourly mark-price candles for BTC-PERP."*

> *"Get 4h mark candles for ETH-PERP."*

**Example response**

```json
[
  {
    "time": "1712696400000",
    "open": "67210.00",
    "high": "67510.00",
    "low": "67090.00",
    "close": "67430.00",
    "volume": "145.23"
  }
]
```

---

# Account Tools

All account tools are free and require no authentication. They use the MCP wallet address by default — pass any Solana address to query other accounts. Results are cached for 5 minutes.

---

## pacifica-account

Get account overview: balance, equity, margin used, available margin, fee level.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `account` | string | No | Solana wallet address. Defaults to MCP wallet. |

**Example prompt**

> *"What's my account balance and available margin?"*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "account": "7xK...abc",
  "balance": "1000.00",
  "equity": "1023.45",
  "margin_used": "67.42",
  "available_margin": "955.58",
  "positions_count": 1,
  "fee_level": "taker"
}
```

---

## pacifica-positions

Get all open positions for an account.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `account` | string | No | Solana wallet address. Defaults to MCP wallet. |

**Example prompt**

> *"Show me my open positions."*

> *"What positions does 7xK...abc have open?"*

**Example response**

```json
[
  {
    "symbol": "BTC-PERP",
    "side": "bid",
    "amount": "0.01",
    "entry_price": "67421.00",
    "mark_price": "67450.00",
    "unrealized_pnl": "0.29",
    "margin": "67.42",
    "funding": "-0.05",
    "leverage": "10"
  }
]
```

---

## pacifica-orders

Get all open orders for an account.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `account` | string | No | Solana wallet address. Defaults to MCP wallet. |

**Example prompt**

> *"Show me my open orders."*

**Example response**

```json
[
  {
    "order_id": "ord_abc123",
    "symbol": "BTC-PERP",
    "side": "bid",
    "amount": "0.05",
    "price": "65000.00",
    "tif": "GTC",
    "status": "open",
    "created_at": "1712699000000"
  }
]
```

---

## pacifica-order-history

Get historical orders for an account.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `account` | string | No | Solana wallet address. Defaults to MCP wallet. |
| `limit` | integer | No | Number of orders, 1–500 (default `50`) |

**Example prompt**

> *"Show me my last 20 orders."*

---

## pacifica-trade-history

Get historical trades with realized PnL and fees.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `account` | string | No | Solana wallet address. Defaults to MCP wallet. |
| `symbol` | string | No | Filter by market (e.g. `BTC-PERP`) |
| `limit` | integer | No | Number of trades, 1–500 (default `50`) |

**Example prompt**

> *"Show me my BTC-PERP trade history with PnL."*

**Example response**

```json
[
  {
    "trade_id": "trd_xyz789",
    "symbol": "BTC-PERP",
    "side": "ask",
    "amount": "0.01",
    "price": "67800.00",
    "realized_pnl": "3.79",
    "fee": "0.034",
    "timestamp": "1712699500000"
  }
]
```

---

## pacifica-portfolio

Get portfolio equity history over time.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `account` | string | No | Solana wallet address. Defaults to MCP wallet. |
| `time_range` | enum | No | `1d`, `7d`, `14d`, `30d`, `all` (default `7d`) |

**Example prompt**

> *"Show me my portfolio performance over the last 30 days."*

**Example response**

```json
[
  { "timestamp": "1712613600000", "equity": "987.00" },
  { "timestamp": "1712700000000", "equity": "1023.45" }
]
```

---

## pacifica-balance-history

Get balance event history: deposits, withdrawals, trades, and funding payments.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `account` | string | No | Solana wallet address. Defaults to MCP wallet. |
| `limit` | integer | No | Number of events, 1–500 (default `50`) |

**Example prompt**

> *"Show me my recent deposits and withdrawals."*

**Example response**

```json
[
  {
    "type": "deposit",
    "amount": "500.00",
    "timestamp": "1712600000000"
  },
  {
    "type": "funding",
    "amount": "-0.05",
    "symbol": "BTC-PERP",
    "timestamp": "1712692800000"
  }
]
```

---

## pacifica-account-settings

Get account settings including margin mode and leverage configuration per market.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `account` | string | No | Solana wallet address. Defaults to MCP wallet. |

**Example prompt**

> *"Show me my margin settings and leverage for each market."*

> *"What leverage is set on my BTC-PERP and ETH-PERP?"*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "margin_settings": [
    {
      "symbol": "BTC-PERP",
      "isolated": false,
      "leverage": 10
    },
    {
      "symbol": "ETH-PERP",
      "isolated": true,
      "leverage": 20
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## pacifica-order-by-id

Get the full event history for a specific order. Returns all lifecycle events — make, fill, partial fill, cancel, etc.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `order_id` | integer | Yes | Order ID |

**Example prompt**

> *"Show me the full history for order 123456."*

> *"What happened to order 789012 — was it filled or canceled?"*

**Example response**

```json
[
  {
    "event": "make",
    "order_id": 123456,
    "price": "65000.00",
    "amount": "0.05",
    "timestamp": "1712699000000"
  },
  {
    "event": "fill",
    "order_id": 123456,
    "price": "65000.00",
    "amount": "0.03",
    "fee": "0.020",
    "timestamp": "1712699500000"
  },
  {
    "event": "cancel",
    "order_id": 123456,
    "remaining_amount": "0.02",
    "timestamp": "1712700000000"
  }
]
```

---

# Trading Tools

Trading tools require a wallet with funds on Pacifica. Every request is signed with your Ed25519 keypair before being sent to the API — Claude never has access to your private key; signing happens inside the MCP server process.

## How signing works

When you ask Claude to place an order, the MCP server:

1. Loads your Ed25519 keypair from `~/.pacifica-mcp/config.json`
2. Builds the request payload with the order parameters
3. Signs the payload with `nacl.sign.detached`
4. Attaches `account`, `signature`, and `timestamp` to the request
5. POSTs to the Pacifica API

The signature proves ownership of the wallet without revealing the private key.

---

## pacifica-market-order

Place a market order. Fills immediately at the best available price.

`bid` = buy / go long. `ask` = sell / go short.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `symbol` | string | Yes | Market symbol (e.g. `BTC-PERP`) |
| `side` | enum | Yes | `bid` (long) or `ask` (short) |
| `amount` | string | Yes | Order size as decimal string (e.g. `"0.01"`) |
| `slippage_percent` | string | No | Max slippage % (default `"1"`) |
| `reduce_only` | boolean | No | Only reduce existing position (default `false`) |

**Example prompt**

> *"Buy 0.01 BTC-PERP at market price."*

> *"Short 0.1 ETH-PERP at market."*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "order_id": "ord_abc123",
  "symbol": "BTC-PERP",
  "side": "bid",
  "amount": "0.01",
  "filled_price": "67421.00",
  "fee": "0.034",
  "status": "filled"
}
```

---

## pacifica-limit-order

Place a limit order at a specific price.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `symbol` | string | Yes | Market symbol |
| `side` | enum | Yes | `bid` (long) or `ask` (short) |
| `amount` | string | Yes | Order size as decimal string |
| `price` | string | Yes | Limit price as decimal string |
| `tif` | enum | No | `GTC` / `IOC` / `ALO` / `TOB` (default `GTC`) |
| `reduce_only` | boolean | No | Only reduce existing position (default `false`) |

**TIF options**

| Value | Description |
|---|---|
| `GTC` | Good-til-cancel — stays open until filled or canceled |
| `IOC` | Immediate-or-cancel — fill what you can, cancel the rest |
| `ALO` | Add-liquidity-only — post-only, rejected if it would take |
| `TOB` | Top-of-book — places at the best bid/ask |

**Example prompt**

> *"Place a buy limit order for 0.05 BTC-PERP at $65,000."*

> *"Sell 0.1 ETH-PERP at $3,100 GTC."*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "order_id": "ord_def456",
  "symbol": "BTC-PERP",
  "side": "bid",
  "amount": "0.05",
  "price": "65000.00",
  "tif": "GTC",
  "status": "open"
}
```

---

## pacifica-stop-order

Place a stop-market or stop-limit order. Triggers when the market reaches `stop_price`.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `symbol` | string | Yes | Market symbol |
| `side` | enum | Yes | `bid` or `ask` |
| `stop_price` | string | Yes | Trigger price as decimal string |
| `amount` | string | Yes | Order size as decimal string |
| `limit_price` | string | No | Limit price after trigger. Omit for stop-market. |
| `reduce_only` | boolean | No | Only reduce position (default `true`) |

**Example prompt**

> *"Place a stop-loss on my BTC-PERP long — sell 0.01 BTC if price drops to $63,000."*

> *"Set a stop-limit order: buy 0.05 ETH-PERP when it hits $3,050, limit at $3,060."*

---

## pacifica-set-tpsl

Set take-profit and/or stop-loss prices on an existing position. Provide at least one of `take_profit_price` or `stop_loss_price`.

> ⚠️ **The `side` parameter is the EXIT side, not the position side.** To set TP/SL on a long (bid) position, use `side: "ask"`. To set TP/SL on a short (ask) position, use `side: "bid"`.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `symbol` | string | Yes | Market symbol |
| `side` | enum | Yes | Exit side: `bid` or `ask` (opposite of your position side) |
| `take_profit_price` | string | No | TP trigger price as decimal string |
| `stop_loss_price` | string | No | SL trigger price as decimal string |

**Example prompt**

> *"Set a take-profit at $72,000 and stop-loss at $63,000 on my BTC-PERP long."*

> *"Add a stop-loss at $2,800 to my ETH-PERP long position."*

---

## pacifica-cancel-order

Cancel a specific order by ID, or cancel all open orders for a symbol.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `symbol` | string | Yes | Market symbol |
| `order_id` | string | No | Order ID to cancel. Omit to cancel all open orders. |

**Example prompt**

> *"Cancel order ord_def456."*

> *"Cancel all my open orders on BTC-PERP."*

**Example response**

```json
{ "canceled": 1 }
```

---

## pacifica-set-leverage

Set the leverage multiplier for a market (1–100x).

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `symbol` | string | Yes | Market symbol |
| `leverage` | integer | Yes | Leverage multiplier, 1–100 |

**Example prompt**

> *"Set my BTC-PERP leverage to 20x."*

> *"Reduce my ETH-PERP leverage to 5x."*

---

## pacifica-set-margin-mode

Switch between cross margin and isolated margin for a market.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `symbol` | string | Yes | Market symbol |
| `is_isolated` | boolean | Yes | `true` = isolated margin, `false` = cross margin |

**Example prompt**

> *"Switch BTC-PERP to isolated margin."*

> *"Enable cross margin on ETH-PERP."*

---

## pacifica-edit-order

Edit an existing order's price and/or amount. The original order is replaced and a new order ID is returned.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `symbol` | string | Yes | Market symbol |
| `price` | string | No | New price as decimal string |
| `amount` | string | No | New amount as decimal string |
| `order_id` | string | Conditional | Order ID to edit. Provide this or `client_order_id`. |
| `client_order_id` | string | Conditional | Client order ID to edit. Provide this or `order_id`. |

**Example prompt**

> *"Change my BTC-PERP limit order ord_def456 to $66,000."*

> *"Update order ord_def456 — set amount to 0.1 and price to $64,500."*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "order_id": "ord_new789",
  "symbol": "BTC-PERP",
  "price": "66000.00",
  "amount": "0.05",
  "status": "open"
}
```

---

## pacifica-batch-order

Submit up to 10 order actions (create and/or cancel) in a single request.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `actions` | array | Yes | JSON array of up to 10 actions (see below) |

Each action object:

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `type` | enum | Yes | `create` or `cancel` |
| `symbol` | string | Yes | Market symbol |
| `side` | enum | For create | `bid` or `ask` |
| `amount` | string | For create | Order size as decimal string |
| `price` | string | For create | Limit price as decimal string |
| `tif` | enum | No | `GTC` / `IOC` / `ALO` / `TOB` (default `GTC`) |
| `order_id` | string | For cancel | Order ID to cancel |

**Example prompt**

> *"Place 3 limit buy orders for BTC-PERP at $64,000, $63,500, and $63,000 — each for 0.01 BTC."*

> *"Cancel orders ord_1 and ord_2, and place a new bid at $65,000 for 0.05 BTC-PERP."*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "results": [
    { "type": "create", "order_id": "ord_batch1", "status": "open" },
    { "type": "create", "order_id": "ord_batch2", "status": "open" },
    { "type": "cancel", "order_id": "ord_1", "status": "canceled" }
  ]
}
```

---

## pacifica-cancel-stop

Cancel a specific stop order by ID.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `symbol` | string | Yes | Market symbol |
| `order_id` | string | Conditional | Stop order ID to cancel. Provide this or `client_order_id`. |
| `client_order_id` | string | Conditional | Client order ID to cancel. Provide this or `order_id`. |

**Example prompt**

> *"Cancel my BTC-PERP stop order ord_stop123."*

> *"Remove the stop-loss order on ETH-PERP."*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "order_id": "ord_stop123",
  "symbol": "BTC-PERP",
  "status": "canceled"
}

---

# Account Management Tools

Account management tools handle withdrawals, subaccount creation, and fund transfers. All operations require wallet signing — the MCP server signs each request with your Ed25519 keypair. Your private key never leaves the local process.

---

## pacifica-withdraw

Withdraw USDC from your Pacifica account to your Solana wallet.

A flat $1 fee is deducted per withdrawal request. Your Solana wallet must already have a USDC token account (ATA) — the tool will not create one.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `amount` | string | Yes | Amount to withdraw as decimal string (min `"1"`, max `"100000"` per day) |

**Example prompt**

> *"Withdraw $500 USDC to my wallet."*

> *"Send 100 USDC back to my Solana wallet."*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "amount": "500.00",
  "fee": "1.00",
  "net_amount": "499.00",
  "status": "pending"
}
```

---

## pacifica-create-subaccount

Create a new subaccount under your main account. A fresh Ed25519 keypair is generated automatically — no parameters needed.

The new subaccount requires a dual signature (main account + subaccount keypair sign together). The subaccount private key is saved locally at `~/.pacifica-mcp/subaccounts/<address>.json` and is **never** exposed through the MCP response or AI output.

**Parameters**

None — the keypair is generated automatically.

**Example prompt**

> *"Create a new subaccount."*

> *"Set up a fresh trading subaccount for me."*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "address": "9xR...def",
  "status": "created",
  "key_path": "~/.pacifica-mcp/subaccounts/9xR...def.json"
}
```

---

## pacifica-list-subaccounts

List all subaccounts under your main account.

Returns on-chain subaccounts with their balances and settings. Also includes locally-saved subaccounts that have not yet appeared on-chain (e.g. recently created, not yet funded).

**Parameters**

None.

**Example prompt**

> *"List all my subaccounts."*

> *"Show me my subaccounts and their balances."*

**Example response**

```json
[
  {
    "address": "9xR...def",
    "balance": "500.00",
    "pending_balance": "0.00",
    "fee_level": "taker",
    "created_at": "1712700000000"
  },
  {
    "address": "4kT...ghi",
    "balance": "0.00",
    "pending_balance": "0.00",
    "fee_level": "taker",
    "created_at": "1712750000000",
    "local_only": true
  }
]
```

---

## pacifica-transfer-funds

Transfer USDC between your main account and a subaccount. Works in both directions — main to sub, or sub to main.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `to_account` | string | Yes | Destination address (subaccount or main account) |
| `amount` | string | Yes | Amount to transfer as decimal string (min `"10"`) |

**Example prompt**

> *"Transfer $200 to my subaccount 9xR...def."*

> *"Move 500 USDC from subaccount 9xR...def back to my main account."*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "from": "7xK...abc",
  "to": "9xR...def",
  "amount": "200.00",
  "status": "completed"
}
```

---

# Real-Time Tools

Real-time tools use WebSocket connections to stream live market and account data. Market channels (prices, trades, orderbook) are free and require no authentication. Account channels (account_info, account_positions, account_trades) require wallet signing.

## Channels reference

| Channel | Requires Symbol | Auth | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `prices` | No | Free | All market prices |
| `trades` | Yes | Free | Trade feed for one market |
| `orderbook` | Yes | Free | Live orderbook updates |
| `account_info` | No | Wallet | Account balance changes |
| `account_positions` | No | Wallet | Position updates |
| `account_trades` | No | Wallet | Your trade fills |

---

## pacifica-watch

Subscribe to a channel, collect events for a duration, and return a summary. This is the simplest way to sample real-time data — no need to manage subscriptions.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `channel` | enum | Yes | `prices`, `trades`, `orderbook`, `account_info`, `account_positions`, `account_trades` |
| `symbol` | string | Conditional | Required for `trades` and `orderbook` channels (e.g. `BTC-PERP`) |
| `duration` | integer | No | Seconds to collect events, 1–60 (default `10`) |

**Example prompt**

> *"Watch BTC-PERP trades for 15 seconds."*

> *"Sample the prices channel for 5 seconds."*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "events": [
    {
      "channel": "trades",
      "symbol": "BTC-PERP",
      "price": "67420.50",
      "amount": "0.025",
      "side": "bid",
      "time": "1712699800000"
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "count": 42,
    "duration_ms": 15000
  }
}
```

---

## pacifica-watch-start

Start a persistent WebSocket subscription that buffers events in the background. Use `pacifica-watch-read` to retrieve buffered events and `pacifica-watch-stop` to end the subscription.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `channel` | enum | Yes | `prices`, `trades`, `orderbook`, `account_info`, `account_positions`, `account_trades` |
| `symbol` | string | Conditional | Required for `trades` and `orderbook` channels (e.g. `ETH-PERP`) |

**Example prompt**

> *"Start streaming BTC-PERP orderbook updates."*

> *"Subscribe to my account position updates."*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "subscription_id": "sub_abc123",
  "channel": "orderbook",
  "symbol": "BTC-PERP",
  "status": "active"
}
```

---

## pacifica-watch-read

Read and drain buffered events from a persistent subscription. The buffer is cleared after reading — each event is returned only once.

> **Tip:** The `prices` channel streams ALL markets simultaneously and can produce massive output. Use `summary_only: true` to get aggregate stats instead of raw events.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `subscription_id` | string | Yes | Subscription ID from `pacifica-watch-start` |
| `summary_only` | boolean | No | Return only summary stats, not raw events (default `false`) |
| `max_events` | integer | No | Maximum events to return, 1–10000 (default `100`) |

**Example prompt**

> *"Read the latest events from subscription sub_abc123."*

> *"Get a summary of the prices subscription."*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "subscription_id": "sub_abc123",
  "events": [
    {
      "channel": "orderbook",
      "symbol": "BTC-PERP",
      "bids": [["67419.0", "1.234"]],
      "asks": [["67421.0", "0.890"]],
      "time": "1712699850000"
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "count": 128,
    "buffer_remaining": 0
  }
}
```

---

## pacifica-watch-stop

Stop a persistent subscription, or stop all active subscriptions at once.

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `subscription_id` | string | No | Subscription ID to stop. Omit to stop all active subscriptions. |

**Example prompt**

> *"Stop subscription sub_abc123."*

> *"Stop all my WebSocket subscriptions."*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "stopped": ["sub_abc123"],
  "remaining_active": []
}
```

---

# System Tools

---

## pacifica-wallet

Show your MCP wallet address, current network (testnet or mainnet), and account balance.

No parameters required.

**Example prompt**

> *"What's my Pacifica wallet address?"*

> *"Show me my wallet info."*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "publicKey": "7xKmN3bPqRsT4uVwXyZ1aB2cD5eF6gH8iJ9kL0mN",
  "network": "testnet",
  "balance": {
    "balance": "1000.00",
    "equity": "1023.45"
  }
}
```

The wallet file is stored at `~/.pacifica-mcp/config.json`. It is auto-created on first use. **Back it up — it contains your private key.**

---

## pacifica-tools

List all available Pacifica MCP tools grouped by category.

No parameters required.

**Example prompt**

> *"What tools do you have for Pacifica?"*

> *"List all available Pacifica MCP tools."*

**Example response**

```json
{
  "Market Data (free, cached)": [
    { "name": "pacifica-markets", "description": "List all markets or look up a single market" },
    { "name": "pacifica-prices", "description": "Real-time prices, funding, open interest, volume" },
    ...
  ],
  "Trading (requires wallet, signed)": [
    { "name": "pacifica-market-order", "description": "Place a market order (bid=long, ask=short)" },
    ...
  ]
}
```

This is a good first tool to call when exploring capabilities.
